summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined a series of recent developments in small UAS operations and countermeasures. Ukrainian forces have used FPV drones to destroy a Russian Ka-27 helicopter launched from a naval drone, marking a first in military history, and have also struck Russian logistics targets up to 50 km behind the front line in Donetsk. Ukraine's CD-T10 radar system now provides 15 km coverage against FPV drones and guided aerial bombs, while the Ukrainian firm has developed the Chipa net gun to physically intercept FPV drones when other defenses fail. The Pentagon has named its first "Drone Dominance" competition winners and selected a Ukrainian FPV drone from F-Drones to arm the US Army, while the US National Guard has purchased three types of FPV drones for military training. Russia is aiming for 101,000 drone troops by April and has tested a "penguin" camouflage suit, though at least two soldiers wearing it were quickly neutralized by FPV drones in snowy conditions.
